meta.title: eCTD Module Mapping Corrections
meta.authority: ICH M4(R4) Organisation of the Common Technical Document — Step 4, June 15, 2016
meta.scope: All rule JSON files from output-v2/ and output-v3/; corrections verified against M4(R4) Tables 1-6 (Granularity Document)

ctd_module_reference.note: Definitive ICH M4(R4) CTD structure — use this as ground truth for all eCTD module assignments

module_1.title: Administrative Information and Prescribing Information
module_1.note: Region-specific. Content and sub-sections defined by each regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, Health Canada). Sub-sections below 1.1/1.2 are NOT harmonized and MUST be labeled as region-specific.

sections.2.3.A: QOS Appendices (A.1 Facilities, A.2 Adventitious Agents, A.3 Excipients)
sections.2.4: Nonclinical Overview (narrative critical analysis of nonclinical program)
sections.2.5: Clinical Overview (narrative critical analysis of clinical program)
sections.2.6.2: Pharmacology Written Summary (primary PD, secondary PD, safety pharmacology, PD interactions)
sections.2.7.1: Summary of Biopharmaceutics Studies and Associated Analytical Methods

sections.3.2.S.3: Characterisation (structure/elucidation, impurity profiling — NOT analytical procedures for control)
sections.3.2.S.4: Control of Drug Substance (specifications, analytical procedures, validation, batch analysis, justification)
sections.3.2.P.2: Pharmaceutical Development (formulation, overages, physicochemical properties, excipients, DP, manufacturing, dissolution, microbiological attributes, compatibility; includes design space, control strategy per Q8/Q11/Q13)
sections.3.2.P.3: Manufacture (manufacturer, batch formula, process description, controls, validation)
sections.3.2.P.5: Control of Drug Product (specifications, analytical procedures, validation, batch analysis, characterization of impurities, justification)
sections.3.2.A.1: Facilities and Equipment (site information, GMP manufacturing)
sections.3.2.A.2: Adventitious Agents Safety Evaluation (per ICH Q5A — viral safety of biotech products)
sections.3.2.A.3: Excipients (novel excipients — human or animal safety data)

sections.4.2.3.5.1: Fertility and early embryonic development (ICH S5)
sections.4.2.3.5.4: Studies in which offspring were dosed

sections.5.2: Tabular Listing of All Clinical Studies
sections.5.3.1.2: Comparative BA and Bioequivalence Study Reports
sections.5.3.1.3: In Vitro-In Vivo Correlation Study Reports
sections.5.3.1.4: Reports of Bioanalytical and Analytical Methods (per ICH M10)
sections.5.3.2: Reports of Studies Pertinent to Pharmacokinetics Using Human Biomaterials
sections.5.3.2.2: Reports of Hepatic Metabolism and Drug Interaction Studies
sections.5.3.2.3: Reports of Studies Using Other Human Biomaterials
sections.5.3.3.1: Healthy Subject PK and Initial Tolerability Study Reports
sections.5.3.3.2: Patient PK and Initial Tolerability Study Reports
sections.5.3.3.3: Intrinsic Factor PK Study Reports (renal/hepatic impairment, age, gender, race)
sections.5.3.3.4: Extrinsic Factor PK Study Reports (drug interactions, food effect, tobacco, antacids)
sections.5.3.4.1: Healthy Subject PD and PK/PD Study Reports
sections.5.3.4.2: Patient PD and PK/PD Study Reports
sections.5.3.5: Reports of Efficacy and Safety Studies
sections.5.3.5.1: Study Reports of Controlled Clinical Trials
sections.5.3.5.2: Study Reports of Uncontrolled Clinical Trials
sections.5.3.5.3: Reports of Analyses of Data from More than One Study (meta-analyses, pooled analyses)
sections.5.3.7: Case Report Forms and Individual Patient Listings

corrections.rule_scope: All stability rules (meta note + all stability data rules)
corrections.original: CTD Module 3.2.P.8 and 3.2.S.7
corrections.corrected: Module 3.2.S.7 (drug substance stability data) AND Module 3.2.P.8 (drug product stability data) — with mandatory cross-reference to Module 2.3.S.7 (QOS drug substance stability summary) and Module 2.3.P.8 (QOS drug product stability summary)

cross_reference.rationale: ICH M4Q requires stability protocol and results summarized in the QOS (Module 2.3); the QOS stability section MUST cross-reference the full data in Module 3.2

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — no ectd_module fields present on any rule
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields on any of the ~87 analytical/impurity rules
corrections.corrected: Each rule category requires the following module assignments:
corrections.m4_basis: M4Q (Quality — CMC) Sections 2.3.S and 2.3.P; M4(R4) Table 2 rows for 3.2.S.3, 3.2.S.4, 3.2.P.5, 3.2.P.7

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — 147 rules, no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module assignments on any of the 147 biotech quality rules (ICH Q5A-Q5E)
corrections.corrected: Required module assignments by Q5 sub-guideline:

viral_safety_Q5A_cell_bank_testing: Module 3.2.A.2 (Adventitious Agents Safety Evaluation — primary location)

corrections.critical_note: Module 3.2.A.2 is THE canonical location for all adventitious agents / viral safety evaluation data per both M4Q and ICH Q5A. This must be the primary module reference for all VS-series rules.

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — Q8-Q13 rules, no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields for Q8-Q13 framework rules
corrections.corrected: Required module assignments by ICH Q-guideline:

pharmaceutical_development_Q8: Module 3.2.P.2 (primary location for all Q8 pharmaceutical development content)

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — S1-S13 nonclinical requirements, no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields on nonclinical study requirements
corrections.corrected: Required module assignments by ICH S-guideline:

study_reports: Module 4.2.2 (nonclinical PK — integrated with toxicology studies)

summary: Module 2.6.2 (Pharmacology Written Summary — safety pharmacology section)

corrections.m4_basis: M4S (Safety — Nonclinical) guideline; M4(R4) Table 5 (Module 4 granularity)

corrections.rule_scope: ctd_placement field (line 425) and all S7A/S7B rules
corrections.original: Module 2.6.2 (Pharmacology Written Summary) and Module 4.2.1.3 (Safety Pharmacology)
corrections.corrected: Module 4.2.1.3 (Safety Pharmacology study reports — primary data location) AND Module 2.6.2 (Pharmacology Written Summary — specifically the safety pharmacology sub-section) AND Module 2.4 (Nonclinical Overview — integrated risk assessment narrative)
corrections.note: Module 2.6.2 includes sub-sections for primary PD, secondary PD, AND safety pharmacology. The safety pharmacology section of 2.6.2 must reference 4.2.1.3. Module 2.4 must provide the integrated risk assessment narrative that incorporates hERG/QTc data.

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — 583 clinical design rules (E1, E3, E4, E7, E8, E9, E10, E20), no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields — rule categories contain ICH guideline references only
corrections.corrected: Required module assignments by ICH E-guideline and rule category:

summary: Module 2.7.4 (Clinical Safety Summary — exposure duration/size tables)

corrections.m4_basis: M4E (Efficacy — Clinical) guideline; M4(R4) Table 6 (Module 5 granularity)

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — E2A-E2F, E14, E19-E23 rules, no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields on any safety reporting rules
corrections.corrected: Required module assignments by rule type:

primary: Module 5.3.7 (individual patient CRFs for fatal/unexpected SAEs)
Module 1 (electronic individual case safety reports — region-specific administrative)
icsr_E2B_r3.note: E2B(R3) ICSRs are submitted as regulatory transactions in Module 1 regional section

primary: Module 2.7.4 (Clinical Safety Summary — basis for PBRER narrative)
psur_pbrer_E2C.note: PSUR/PBRER itself is a standalone submission document but references Module 5.3.6 and Module 2.7.4 content

primary: Module 5.3.3.4 (extrinsic factor PK — drug interaction on QTc)
summary: Module 2.7.2 (Clinical Pharmacology Summary — cardiac safety sub-section)

corrections.rule_scope: 412 rules — E5, E6, E11, E11A, E12, E15, E16, E17, E18, no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields on any special populations rules
corrections.corrected: Required module assignments by guideline:

primary: Module 5.3.3.3 (intrinsic factor PK — race/ethnic PK differences)
summary: Module 2.7.2 (Clinical Pharmacology — PK in new region)

primary: Module 5 (applies to conduct and documentation of all clinical studies)
gcp_E6.note: GCP is a framework that applies to all of Module 5; no single CTD section

corrections.original: Domain-level coverage noted but no per-rule ectd_module fields
corrections.corrected: Required module assignments by ICH M-guideline:

primary: Module 5.3.5 (adverse event coding in trial reports)
All modules (data standards for eCTD content)
Module 3.2.S.4.5 (justification of specification limits for DS)
Module 3.2.P.5.6 (justification of specification limits for DP)
Module 4.2.3.7.6 (toxicology of impurities — if separate tox study)
All modules (M8 governs the electronic format of the entire CTD)
ectd_submission_M8.note: M8 rules apply to all five modules; the eCTD_module field for M8 rules should reference the specific module being formatted/submitted

primary: Module 5.3.1.2 (bioequivalence study reports — or waiver justification)

bioanalytical_validation_M10.note: M10 bioanalytical validation reports go in Module 5.3.1.4 for clinical studies and Module 4.2.2.1 for nonclinical

clinical_protocol_M11.note: CeSHarP-structured protocols are submitted as part of the study report in Module 5.3.5

summary: Module 2.6.4 (Nonclinical PK Written Summary — DDI section)

primary: Module 5.3.1.2 (comparative BA and BE study reports)
Module 3.2.P.2 (pharma development — dissolution and BE correlation)
Module 5.3.5.3 (analyses from multiple studies / RWE analyses)

corrections.m4_basis: M4E, M4Q, M4S; M4(R4) Tables 5 and 6

corrections.rule_scope: All rules using abbreviated 'MX.Y.Z' format without 'Module' prefix
corrections.original: Uses abbreviated format: 'M1', 'M3.2.P.8', 'M5.3.1.2', 'M4.2.2.2', etc.
corrections.corrected: All module references MUST use format: 'Module 1', 'Module 3.2.P.8', 'Module 5.3.1.2', 'Module 4.2.2.2'
corrections.standardization_rule: Prefix 'M' is an internal shorthand only. Public-facing rule JSON must use full 'Module X.Y.Z' notation to match the ICH M4(R4) document and eCTD submission tooling conventions.

valid_abbreviated_forms: None — all must use full 'Module X.Y.Z' format

corrections.rule_scope: Rules using 'M1.8.2', 'M1.12', 'M1.14', 'M1.18.1' without qualifier
corrections.original: "ectd_module": "M1.8.2" — no qualifier; implies harmonized CTD section
corrections.corrected: Must add jurisdiction qualifier: e.g., "Module 1.8.2 (FDA-specific)" or "Module 1.14 (FDA-specific — labeling)"

affected_rules.corrected: Module 1.8.2 (FDA-specific — CDER proprietary name review or labeling sub-section)
Module 1.14 (FDA-specific — proposed labeling: PI, PPI, MedGuide)

corrections.rationale: ICH M4(R4) explicitly states Module 1 is region-specific. Sub-sections of Module 1 beyond 1.1 and 1.2 are defined by each regulatory authority. Using region-specific sub-section numbers without a qualifier creates confusion about applicability.
corrections.m4_basis: M4(R4) page 2: 'Module 1 is region specific. The content and format of this module can be specified by the relevant regulatory authorities.'

corrections.rule_scope: Rules using 'M3.2.A' without sub-section specification
corrections.original: "ectd_module": "M3.2.A" (used for manufacturing/facility/GMP deficiencies)
corrections.corrected: Must specify which appendix: Module 3.2.A.1 (Facilities and Equipment), Module 3.2.A.2 (Adventitious Agents), or Module 3.2.A.3 (Novel Excipients)

corrections.rule_scope: One rule using 'M3.2.P.5/M3.2.S.3' for analytical method context
corrections.original: "ectd_module": "M3.2.P.5/M3.2.S.3" — implies analytical control data in Module 3.2.S.3
corrections.corrected: Depends on content type: (A) For analytical procedure validation of drug substance: Module 3.2.S.4.3 — NOT 3.2.S.3. (B) For impurity structure elucidation/identification: Module 3.2.S.3.2 is correct.
corrections.rule: IF the CRL deficiency concerns 'analytical method validation' or 'specifications' → Module 3.2.S.4 (NOT S.3). IF the deficiency concerns 'impurity identification/structure' → Module 3.2.S.3 is correct.

corrections.rule_scope: Rules using range notation 'M3-M5', 'M1-M5', 'M1-M3, M5.3.1'
corrections.original: "ectd_module": "M3-M5" or "M1-M5" or "M3/M4/M5"
corrections.corrected: Replace range notation with specific module list or use descriptive phrase with specific cross-references

examples.corrected: Module 3 (Quality), Module 4 (Nonclinical), Module 5 (Clinical) — specify applicable sub-sections per rule
All modules — use only when rule genuinely applies equally to all five modules (e.g., GCP, data integrity)
Module 3.2.P.2, Module 4.2.3, Module 5.3.5 — specify the relevant sub-sections

corrections.rationale: Range notation obscures which specific section of the dossier should be checked, defeating the purpose of the module mapping.

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — 412 requirements, no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields
corrections.corrected: EU legislation requirements map to CTD modules as follows:

primary: Module 1 (EU-specific: application form, product info, labeling)
Module 1 (EU-specific — RMP as EU Module 1 section)

corrections.rule_scope: Line 781 uses only 'Module 3' for process validation — too vague
corrections.original: "Module 3" for process validation requirements
corrections.corrected: Module 3.2.P.3 (Manufacture — process validation) AND Module 3.2.P.2 (Pharmaceutical Development — process design, validation strategy) AND Module 3.2.A.1 (Facilities and Equipment — GMP site description)

corrections.rule_scope: Entire file — EU GVP Module rules, no ectd_module fields
corrections.original: No ectd_module fields
corrections.corrected: EU pharmacovigilance GVP modules map to CTD as follows:

primary: Module 1 (EU RMP as regional Module 1 section)
Module 2.7.4 (clinical safety summary — risk characterization basis)
Module 5.3.5 (PASS study reports as clinical study)

corrections.rule_scope: Module references inconsistently describe CTD structure
corrections.original: Uses 'Module 1 (Administrative/Regional)', 'Module 2', 'Module 3 (Quality)' — inconsistent with granularity level
corrections.corrected: Health Canada uses the ICH CTD with Canada-specific Module 1. Rules should specify both the harmonized module (Module 2-5) AND the Canada-specific Module 1 section where applicable.

specific_corrections.corrected: Module 1.5 (Health Canada-specific — Environmental Assessment Statement per CEPA)
Module 1.2.8 (Health Canada-specific — comparative bioavailability waiver justification)
Module 2.3 (Quality Overall Summary — required for CS-BE applications); Module 2.1 (CTD ToC)

corrections.rule_scope: Japan/PMDA rules use 'Section X: CTD Module X' terminology — correct but inconsistent format
corrections.original: Uses 'Section 1: Regional administrative information (CTD Module 1)' etc.
corrections.corrected: Standardize to: 'Module 1 (Japan-specific — regional administrative information per PMDA)', 'Module 2 (CTD Summaries)', etc.
corrections.note: Content is substantively correct. Japan uses the ICH CTD format with Japan-specific Module 1 per PMDA requirements. The 'Section' terminology reflects historical PMDA usage and should be mapped to official CTD module numbers.
